game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Noita is a magical action roguelite set in a world where every pixel is physically simulated. Fight, explore, melt, burn, freeze and evaporate your way through the procedurally generated world using spells you've created yourself. Noita is a magical action roguelite set in a world where every pixel is physically simulated. Fight, explore, melt, burn, freeze and evaporate your way through the procedurally generated world using spells you've created yourself. Explore a variety of environments ranging from coal mines to freezing wastelands while delving deeper in search for unknown mysteries.

  • Pixel-based physics: Every pixel in the world is simulated. Burn, explode or melt anything. Swim in the blood of your foes! Enter a simulated world that is more interactive than anything you've seen before.

  • Your own magic: Combine spells to create your own magic as you delve deeper into the caverns. Use magic to crush your enemies and manipulate the world around you.

  • Procedurally generated world: Explore a unique world every time you play. Discover new environments as you adventure deeper.



  • Action roguelite: Death is permanent and always a looming threat. When you die, don’t despair, use what you’ve learned to get further on your next adventure.

Noita is being developed by Nolla Games, a company set up by 3 indie developers, all of whom have worked on their own projects in the past.

The three gents in question are:

Petri Purho
Petri is best known as the creator of Crayon Physics Deluxe. In his youth he also made a lot tiny freeware games. He has also made a bunch of board games, but he hasn't told about it to anyone. So please keep it a secret.

Olli Harjola
Olli is known for the award-winning, mind-bending sci-fi puzzler The Swapper. Besides indie games, he also dabbles in music, live visuals and making programming languages.

Arvi Teikari
Arvi is also known as Hempuli. Over the years he has released a lot of tiny experimental games, so many in fact that he is unaware of all of them. One of the games he remembers making is Baba Is You.
game_info.description: Descend into a mystical cavern where every pixel is alive with elemental magic. As a powerful wizard, you must navigate through procedurally generated chambers filled with enemies and destructible terrain. Collect spell components to craft powerful magic combinations - burn through obstacles, freeze enemies, or create explosive chain reactions. Each element interacts realistically: water extinguishes fire, fire melts ice, and explosions scatter everything. Survive the descent, defeat enemies, and reach the deepest chamber to claim victory. Death is permanent, but knowledge persists.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move left/right, fall through platforms
Space: Cast primary spell
Shift: Cast secondary spell (when unlocked)
Z: Switch active spell component
ENTER: Start game
ESC: Pause/Unpause
R: Return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement mechanics and collision detection. Validates that the player can move through the environment, interact with platforms, and navigate the level structure without getting stuck.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the player through different parts of the level using arrow keys. Test falling through platforms, walking on solid ground, and basic movement responsiveness. Ensure collision detection works properly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player should move smoothly without passing through solid terrain. Collision boundaries should be respected. Movement should feel responsive and natural. Test passes if player can navigate without errors.

TEST_1.strategy: Move the player through different parts of the level using arrow keys. Test falling through platforms, walking on solid ground, and basic movement responsiveness. Ensure collision detection works properly.
TEST_1.expected: Player should move smoothly without passing through solid terrain. Collision boundaries should be respected. Movement should feel responsive and natural. Test passes if player can navigate without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the complete win condition by implementing an optimal strategy. Validates spell casting, enemy combat, element collection, and progression through the cavern to reach the final goal.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Navigate downward through chambers while collecting spell elements. Use fire spells to destroy wooden obstacles and burn enemies. Collect power-ups to enhance abilities. Strategically combat enemies while managing health. Reach the bottom chamber to trigger win condition.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player successfully reaches the deepest chamber and triggers GAME_OVER_WIN state. All game mechanics work correctly including spell casting, element interactions, enemy AI, and win detection. Test is successful when win screen appears.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests spell crafting system and element interactions. Validates that different spell components can be collected and combined, and that elemental physics work as expected.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately collect different spell elements and test each combination. Cast fire near water to test extinguishing. Use ice spells near enemies. Test explosion chain reactions with multiple elements.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: All spell combinations work as designed. Element interactions follow logical physics rules. Player can successfully switch between collected spell types. Visual feedback clearly shows element effects.
TEST_3.description: Tests spell crafting system and element interactions. Validates that different spell components can be collected and combined, and that elemental physics work as expected.
TEST_3.strategy: Deliberately collect different spell elements and test each combination. Cast fire near water to test extinguishing. Use ice spells near enemies. Test explosion chain reactions with multiple elements.
TEST_3.expected: All spell combinations work as designed. Element interactions follow logical physics rules. Player can successfully switch between collected spell types. Visual feedback clearly shows element effects.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests enemy AI behavior and combat mechanics. Validates that enemies spawn correctly, follow intelligent behavior patterns, and interact properly with player spells and the environment.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Engage with different enemy types using various spell combinations. Test enemy pathfinding, attack patterns, and response to player actions. Verify that defeated enemies grant appropriate rewards.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Enemies behave intelligently and provide appropriate challenge. Combat feels balanced and fair. Enemy AI responds logically to player position and spells. Rewards are granted correctly upon enemy defeat.

TEST_5.test_description: Tests game over conditions and health management. Validates that player takes appropriate damage, health system works correctly, and lose condition triggers properly.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Intentionally take damage from enemies and environmental hazards. Monitor health depletion. Allow health to reach zero to trigger game over state. Verify proper state transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Health decreases appropriately from damage sources. Visual feedback clearly shows health status. Game over triggers correctly at zero health. Lose screen displays with proper restart option.
TEST_5.description: Tests game over conditions and health management. Validates that player takes appropriate damage, health system works correctly, and lose condition triggers properly.
